title: practical english usage - Part 363
content:
You're not going home until you've finished that report.

Present perfect and past perfect tenses can emphasise the idea of completion.

I'll wait until she gets here, (     ... until-she will get-here.) Present tenses are used to refer to the future after until (►  23.1).

4 tenses     with    until You can earn up to £1,500  a week in this job.

The minibus can hold up to thirteen people, (     .. .-imtit-thirteen-people/) not We walked as far as!up to the edge of the forest, (    . .. till-the edge .. .) as far as or up to; up to is also used to talk about quantity.

Until/till is used only to talk about time. To  talk about distance, we  use to, not  used 3 distance      and   quantity:     until/till ►     607.

For American English from .. . through, six o`dwk. . .) not I waited for her until six o'clock, but she didn't come, (     I waited for her to In other cases, to is not generally used.

It's another three weeks to my birthday, (   ... until/till my birthday.) We can also use to when counting the time until a future event.

I usually work from nine to five, (   .. .from nine until/till five.) as until/till. This happens after from ...

To can sometimes be used as a preposition of time with the same meaning 2  until/till        and   to The new timetable will remain in operation until June 30.

I'll wait until/ till I hear from you.

OK,  then, I won't expect you until/till midnight.

exactly the same. Till (AmE also 'til or til) is informal.

These two words can be used both as prepositions and conjunctions. They  mean 1  until     and   till 613 until ►     111 the    British     Isles and (the)   United       Kingdom,       England,       Britain ►    592 and type     of,   kind     of      sort    of Note the preposition: on a journey/trip/voyage.

A  long sea journey is often called a voyage.

hh-trip to the South Pole ...) not Amundsen made his journey to the South Pole in 1911. (     Amundsen m In 1863 the President travelled to Dakota to make peace with the Indians.

Word    Problems      from    A to  Z 614 vocabulary7 •         up, down The nearest bank is about half a mile up/down the road.

or no difference of meaning.

Sometimes both up and down are used to mean 'along', 'further on', with little 3  'along' 1 work in London, but I have to travel up to Glasgow every few weeks.

(perhaps because north is at the top of a map page).

People often use up and down for movements towards the north and south 2  north     and   south But in the US downtown refers to the central business/entertainment area.

We’ll be going down to the country for the weekend.

She ran down the passage, out of the front door and down the garden.

The ambassador walked slowly up the room towards the Queen’s throne.

be called 'up trains’ , and trains from London 'down trains'.) also refer to more or less important or central places. (Trains to London used to Up and down are not only used to refer to higher and lower positions. They  can 1  'towards/away         from    the   centre' 614 up,   down There’s only six weeks left until/before Christmas.

It’ll be ages until/before we meet again.

And both until and before can be used to say how  far away a future event is.

I won’t be seeing Judy until/before Tuesday.

Not until/till can mean the same as not before.

7 until     and   before not No, but we can do it by next Tuesday, (     ... until next Tuesday.) - Can you repair my watch if I leave it until Saturday?

(= at twelve on Monday or before) Yes, but you'll have to leave by twelve on Monday at the latest.

- Can 1 stay until the weekend?

before a future moment. Compare: moment. We   use by (►  414) to say that an action or event will happen at or We   use until to talk about a situation or state that will continue up to a certain states and actions 6 until     and   by: Not until 1 left home did I begin to understand how strange my family was.

Not until that evening was she able to recover her self-control.

inverted word order in the main clause (►  270).

In a literary style it is possible to begin a sentence with Not until. . ., using 5 structure       with    Not   until     .. .
